start.speaker: Prudence
start.text: Do you not still, at times, feel the country you came from pulling at you — the old thoughts, the old comforts?
start.text_zh: 你是否仍会时时感到你所离开的那地在拉扯你——旧日的念头，旧日的安逸？

choices.text: I do. But when they come, I remember the Cross and they lose their hold.
choices.text_zh: 是的。但它们来时，我就记起十字架，它们便抓不住我了。

choices.text: Often. Some days the pull is very strong.
choices.text_zh: 常常。有些日子，那拉力很强。

counsel.speaker: Prudence
counsel.text: Then keep watch over your heart. When the valley accuses you, do not argue in your own strength — answer with the promise. That is the wisdom that keeps a pilgrim.
counsel.text_zh: 那就看守你的心。当那幽谷控告你时，不要凭自己的力量争辩——以应许回答。这就是保守朝圣者的智慧。

choices.text: I will keep watch. Thank you, Prudence.
